message.content:
You are implementing Tickets 6-10 for the OmegA Architecture project at /home/mega/OmegA-Architecture. Write ALL these files with REAL implementations (no placeholders, no TODOs).

EXISTING CODEBASE CONTEXT:
- omega/answer.py has AnswerObject (answer_id, text, citations, confidence, uncertainty_flag, verifier, mode, unresolved_questions), AnswerBuilder, AnswerMode (GROUNDED/INFERRED/ABSTAINED/BOUNDED), Citation, VerifierOutput
- omega/retrieval.py has RetrievedChunk (chunk_id, doc_id, content, context, score, source_ref, doc_hash, section, chunk_index, retrieval_method), RetrievalResult, HybridRetriever, RetrievalMethod
- omega/drift.py has DriftController, GoalContract (task, scope, constraints, success_criteria, assumptions, unknowns), ClaimBudget, Claim, SupportStatus, VerifierMiddleware
- omega/memory.py has MemoryGraph, MemoryNode, EdgeBundle, WritePolicy (CANONICAL/WORKING/INFERRED/SPECULATIVE), Stratum
- omega/envelope.py has RunEnvelope, ActionGate, ActionEnvelope, ActionClass (READ/WRITE/EXECUTE/DELETE/NETWORK/AUTH), ApprovalStatus, ApprovalSource, ApprovalRecord
- omega/risk_gate.py has RiskGate (score, gate, is_policy_blocked, multi_gate)
- omega/runtime.py has RuntimeOrchestrator (run method with stages: RISK_GATE, RETRIEVE, PLAN, GENERATE, VERIFY, ANSWER_BUILD, ACTION_GATE, COMMIT), RuntimeResult, RunTrace, StageTrace, RuntimeStage
- omega/phylactery.py has Phylactery, PhylacteryCommit
- omega/capabilities.py has CapabilityRegistry, CapabilityManifest, RiskClass
- omega/agent.py has OmegaAgent
- All use dataclasses (not pydantic), to_dict() pattern, str Enums

TICKET 6 — CLAIM GRAPH + EVIDENCE LINKING:
Write omega/claims.py with:
- ClaimType enum: SUPPORTED, INFERRED, CONTRADICTED, UNRESOLVED
- ClaimNode dataclass: claim_id, text, claim_type, support_strength (0-1), grounding_strength (0-1), source_refs list, created_at, to_dict()
- EvidenceEdge dataclass: edge_id, source_claim_id, target_claim_id, relation (SUPPORTS/CONTRADICTS/QUALIFIES/SUPERSEDES), weight (0-1), source_ref, excerpt, to_dict()
- ClaimGraph class:
  - claims dict, edges list
  - add_claim(), add_edge(), get_contradictions(), get_unresolved(), grounding_ratio()
  - propagate_uncertainty() — reduce support_strength of claims contradicted by other claims
  - to_dict()
  - from_retrieval_and_answer(answer: AnswerObject, chunks: list[RetrievedChunk]) -> ClaimGraph — static/class method that builds a claim graph from an answer + chunks

Also modify omega/answer.py:
- Add optional claim_graph field to AnswerObject (ClaimGraph | None = None)
- Include it in to_dict() if present

Write schemas/claim_node.schema.json, schemas/evidence_edge.schema.json, schemas/claim_graph.schema.json

TICKET 7 — QUERY PLANNER + RETRIEVAL STRATEGY:
Write omega/query_planner.py with:
- QueryStrategy enum: SIMPLE, MULTI_HOP, CONTRADICTION_AWARE, EXHAUSTIVE
- QueryPlan dataclass: plan_id, original_query, rewritten_queries list, strategy, lexical_weight, vector_weight, metadata_filters dict, top_k, max_hops, escalation_threshold, created_at, to_dict()
- QueryPlanner class:
  - plan(query, task_class="research") -> QueryPlan
  - _rewrite_query(query) -> list of rewritten queries (synonym expansion, decomposition)
  - _select_strategy(query, task_class) -> QueryStrategy
  - _plan_filters(query) -> dict of metadata filters

Modify omega/retrieval.py:
- Add execute_plan(plan: QueryPlan) -> RetrievalResult method to HybridRetriever
- Multi-hop: iteratively retrieve, extract follow-up terms, re-retrieve
- Contradiction-aware: retrieve, check for conflicting chunks, re-retrieve to resolve

Write schemas/query_plan.schema.json

TICKET 8 — SESSION CONTINUITY STORE:
Write omega/session.py with:
- SessionStatus enum: ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, RESUMED, CRASHED, COMPLETED
- SessionState dataclass: session_id, run_ids list, phylactery_head, memory_snapshot dict, task_summaries list, status, created_at, updated_at, version int, to_dict()
- SessionSnapshot dataclass: snapshot_id, session_id, state (SessionState serialized as dict), created_at, to_dict()
- ContinuitySummary dataclass: summary_id, session_id, text, claims_made int, evidence_cited int, unresolved list, created_at, to_dict()
- SessionStore class:
  - __init__(self, persist_dir: str | None = None)
  - save(state: SessionState) -> SessionSnapshot
  - load(session_id: str) -> SessionState | None
  - resume(session_id: str) -> SessionState — loads + marks RESUMED
  - crash_recover(session_id: str) -> SessionState — loads crashed session, marks RESUMED
  - list_sessions() -> list[dict]
  - _persist(snapshot: SessionSnapshot) — writes to disk if persist_dir set

Write schemas/session_state.schema.json, schemas/session_snapshot.schema.json

TICKET 9 — TASK OBJECT MODEL:
Write omega/tasks.py with:
- TaskStatus enum: QUEUED, RUNNING, BLOCKED, AWAITING_APPROVAL, COMPLETED, FAILED
- TaskTransition dataclass: from_status, to_status, reason, timestamp, to_dict()
- TaskObject dataclass: task_id, parent_id (str|None), objective, constraints list, success_criteria list, unknowns list, dependencies list[str], status, transitions list[TaskTransition], outputs list[dict], run_ids list[str], created_at, updated_at, to_dict()
  - transition(new_status, reason) method — validates legal transitions, appends to transitions list
  - add_output(output: dict) method
  - is_terminal property
- TaskRegistry class:
  - tasks dict
  - create(objective, parent_id=None, constraints=None, ...) -> TaskObject
  - get(task_id) -> TaskObject | None
  - transition(task_id, new_status, reason) -> TaskTransition
  - children(task_id) -> list[TaskObject]
  - active_tasks() -> list[TaskObject]
  - to_dict() -> dict summary

Write schemas/task_object.schema.json, schemas/task_transition.schema.json

TICKET 10 — PLANNER / EXECUTOR SPLIT:
Write omega/planner.py with:
- PlanStep dataclass: step_id, action, description, inputs dict, expected_output str, risk_class (from RiskClass), requires_approval bool, to_dict()
- PlanObject dataclass: plan_id, task_id, steps list[PlanStep], validated bool, validation_errors list[str], created_at, to_dict()
- Planner class:
  - plan(task: TaskObject, context: dict = None) -> PlanObject
  - validate(plan: PlanObject) -> PlanObject — sets validated=True if valid, populates validation_errors if not
  - _estimate_risk(step: PlanStep) -> float
  - _requires_approval(step: PlanStep) -> bool

Write omega/executor.py with:
- ExecutionResult dataclass: step_id, status (success/failed/blocked/skipped), output, error, elapsed_ms, to_dict()
- Executor class:
  - execute(plan: PlanObject, runtime: RuntimeOrchestrator) -> list[ExecutionResult]
  - _execute_step(step: PlanStep, runtime) -> ExecutionResult
  - Refuses to execute unvalidated plans (raises ValueError)
  - Halts on approval-required steps (returns BLOCKED result)

Write schemas/plan_object.schema.json

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Use dataclasses, not pydantic
- Use str Enums where appropriate
- Include to_dict() on all dataclasses
- Use uuid.uuid4() for IDs
- Use time.time() for timestamps
- Import from omega.* modules as needed
- Keep files self-contained where possible
- No external dependencies beyond stdlib
- No TODOs or placeholders
- Write real logic, not stubs

1→"""
     2→ADCCL Answer Object — Source-grounded response with citations and uncertainty.
     3→
     4→Every answer produced by OmegA carries:
     5→  - answer text
     6→  - citations (source references + excerpts)
     7→  - confidence score
     8→  - uncertainty flag
     9→  - unresolved questions
    10→  - verifier output
    11→  - mode (grounded / inferred / abstained / bounded)
    12→
    13→Low-evidence mode degrades gracefully: abstain, ask for source,
    14→or offer bounded inference rather than hallucinating.
    15→
    16→Architecture: ADCCL Phase 4
    17→"""
    18→
    19→import time
    20→import uuid
    21→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    22→from enum import Enum
    23→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
    24→
    25→if TYPE_CHECKING:
    26→    from omega.retrieval import RetrievedChunk
    27→
    28→
    29→class AnswerMode(str, Enum):
    30→    GROUNDED = "grounded"      # citations cover the claim
    31→    INFERRED = "inferred"      # reasoning from context, partial support
    32→    ABSTAINED = "abstained"    # insufficient evidence — no answer given
    33→    BOUNDED = "bounded"        # explicit uncertainty range offered
    34→
    35→
    36→@dataclass
    37→class Citation:
    38→    source_ref: str
    39→    chunk_id: str
    40→    doc_hash: str | None = None
    41→    excerpt: str = ""
    42→    relevance_score: float = 0.0
    43→
    44→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    45→        return {
    46→            "source_ref": self.source_ref,
    47→            "chunk_id": self.chunk_id,
    48→            "doc_hash": self.doc_hash,
    49→            "excerpt": self.excerpt[:200],
    50→            "relevance_score": round(self.relevance_score, 4),
    51→        }
    52→
    53→
    54→@dataclass
    55→class VerifierOutput:
    56→    V: float                         # composite verification score [0,1]
    57→    passed: bool
    58→    outcome: str                     # verified | uncertain | rejected
    59→    grounding_ratio: float = 0.0
    60→    hallucination_signals: int = 0
    61→    hedge_signals: int = 0
    62→    goal_relevance: float = 0.0
    63→
    64→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    65→        return {
    66→            "V": round(self.V, 4),
    67→            "passed": self.passed,
    68→            "outcome": self.outcome,
    69→            "grounding_ratio": round(self.grounding_ratio, 4),
    70→            "hallucination_signals": self.hallucination_signals,
    71→            "hedge_signals": self.hedge_signals,
    72→            "goal_relevance": round(self.goal_relevance, 4),
    73→        }
    74→
    75→
    76→@dataclass
    77→class AnswerObject:
    78→    """
    79→    Canonical OmegA answer: structured, cited, and verifier-stamped.
    80→    This is the machine-facing form — human surfaces render from this.
    81→    """
    82→    answer_id: str
    83→    text: str
    84→    citations: list[Citation]
    85→    confidence: float
    86→    uncertainty_flag: bool
    87→    verifier: VerifierOutput
    88→    mode: AnswerMode = AnswerMode.GROUNDED
    89→    unresolved_questions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    90→    generated_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    91→
    92→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    93→        return {
    94→            "answer_id": self.answer_id,
    95→            "text": self.text,
    96→            "citations": [c.to_dict() for c in self.citations],
    97→            "confidence": round(self.confidence, 4),
    98→            "uncertainty_flag": self.uncertainty_flag,
    99→            "unresolved_questions": self.unresolved_questions,
   100→            "verifier": self.verifier.to_dict(),
   101→            "mode": self.mode.value,
   102→            "generated_at": self.generated_at,
   103→        }
   104→
   105→    @property
   106→    def is_trustworthy(self) -> bool:
   107→        """True only when verifier passed and grounding ratio is non-trivial."""
   108→        return self.verifier.passed and self.verifier.grounding_ratio > 0.3
   109→
   110→
   111→class AnswerBuilder:
   112→    """
   113→    Constructs AnswerObjects from raw text + retrieved chunks.
   114→    Enforces the low-evidence degradation policy.
   115→
   116→    Policy:
   117→      grounding_ratio >= 0.5  → GROUNDED
   118→      grounding_ratio >= 0.2  → INFERRED + uncertainty_flag
   119→      grounding_ratio < 0.2   → ABSTAINED (text replaced with abstention)
   120→    """
   121→
   122→    GROUNDED_THRESHOLD = 0.5
   123→    INFERRED_THRESHOLD = 0.2
   124→    CONFIDENCE_SCALE = 0.8   # max confidence when grounded
   125→
   126→    def build(
   127→        self,
   128→        query: str,
   129→        raw_text: str,
   130→        retrieved_chunks: list["RetrievedChunk"],
   131→        verifier_dict: dict | None = None,
   132→        unresolved: list[str] | None = None,
   133→    ) -> AnswerObject:
   134→        """
   135→        Build an AnswerObject. Applies degradation policy automatically.
   136→
   137→        raw_text: text produced by the model
   138→        retrieved_chunks: RetrievedChunk list from HybridRetriever
   139→        verifier_dict: output of DriftController.verify()
   140→        """
   141→        citations = self._build_citations(retrieved_chunks)
   142→        grounding_ratio = self._grounding_ratio(retrieved_chunks)
   143→
   144→        verifier = self._build_verifier(
   145→            verifier_dict or {}, grounding_ratio
   146→        )
   147→
   148→        mode, text, uncertainty_flag, confidence = self._apply_policy(
   149→            raw_text, grounding_ratio, verifier
   150→        )
   151→
   152→        return AnswerObject(
   153→            answer_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
   154→            text=text,
   155→            citations=citations,
   156→            confidence=confidence,
   157→            uncertainty_flag=uncertainty_flag,
   158→            verifier=verifier,
   159→            mode=mode,
   160→            unresolved_questions=unresolved or [],
   161→        )
   162→
   163→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   164→    # Internals
   165→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   166→
   167→    def _build_citations(self, chunks: list["RetrievedChunk"]) -> list[Citation]:
   168→        seen = set()
   169→        result = []
   170→        for chunk in chunks:
   171→            if chunk.chunk_id in seen:
   172→                continue
   173→            seen.add(chunk.chunk_id)
   174→            result.append(Citation(
   175→                source_ref=chunk.source_ref,
   176→                chunk_id=chunk.chunk_id,
   177→                doc_hash=chunk.doc_hash,
   178→                excerpt=chunk.content[:200],
   179→                relevance_score=chunk.score,
   180→            ))
   181→        return result
   182→
   183→    def _grounding_ratio(self, chunks: list["RetrievedChunk"]) -> float:
   184→        if not chunks:
   185→            return 0.0
   186→        grounded = sum(1 for c in chunks if c.score > 0.1)
   187→        return grounded / len(chunks)
   188→
   189→    def _build_verifier(self, v: dict, grounding_ratio: float) -> VerifierOutput:
   190→        V = v.get("V", 0.0)
   191→        # Boost V by grounding evidence
   192→        V_adjusted = min(1.0, V * 0.7 + grounding_ratio * 0.3)
   193→        passed = V_adjusted > 0.4
   194→        if not passed:
   195→            outcome = "uncertain"
   196→        elif grounding_ratio < self.INFERRED_THRESHOLD:
   197→            outcome = "uncertain"
   198→        else:
   199→            outcome = "verified"
   200→        return VerifierOutput(
   201→            V=V_adjusted,
   202→            passed=passed,
   203→            outcome=outcome,
   204→            grounding_ratio=grounding_ratio,
   205→            hallucination_signals=v.get("hallucination_signals", 0),
   206→            hedge_signals=v.get("hedge_signals", 0),
   207→            goal_relevance=v.get("goal_relevance", 0.0),
   208→        )
   209→
   210→    def _apply_policy(
   211→        self,
   212→        raw_text: str,
   213→        grounding_ratio: float,
   214→        verifier: VerifierOutput,
   215→    ) -> tuple[AnswerMode, str, bool, float]:
   216→        """Returns (mode, text, uncertainty_flag, confidence)."""
   217→
   218→        if grounding_ratio >= self.GROUNDED_THRESHOLD and verifier.passed:
   219→            return (
   220→                AnswerMode.GROUNDED,
   221→                raw_text,
   222→                False,
   223→                self.CONFIDENCE_SCALE * grounding_ratio,
   224→            )
   225→        elif grounding_ratio >= self.INFERRED_THRESHOLD:
   226→            caveat = (
   227→                "\n\n[Note: This answer is based on partial evidence. "
   228→                "Confidence is limited — treat as inference, not fact.]"
   229→            )
   230→            return (
   231→                AnswerMode.INFERRED,
   232→                raw_text + caveat,
   233→                True,
   234→                self.CONFIDENCE_SCALE * grounding_ratio * 0.5,
   235→            )
   236→        else:
   237→            # Abstain — do not emit the model's raw text
   238→            abstention = (
   239→                "Insufficient source evidence to answer this question confidently. "
   240→                "Please provide a source document or rephrase with more context."
   241→            )
   242→            return (
   243→                AnswerMode.ABSTAINED,
   244→                abstention,
   245→                True,
   246→                0.0,
   247→            )
   248→

1→"""
     2→MYELIN Hybrid Retrieval — Vector + Lexical + Metadata search
     3→with context expansion and source grounding.
     4→
     5→Without an embedding backend, falls back to TF-IDF-style lexical scoring.
     6→When an embedding function is injected, enables semantic vector search.
     7→Hybrid mode combines both with configurable weights.
     8→
     9→Architecture: MYELIN Phase 3
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import math
    13→import re
    14→import time
    15→from collections import Counter
    16→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    17→from enum import Enum
    18→from typing import Callable
    19→
    20→from omega.docstore import DocumentChunk, DocumentStore
    21→
    22→
    23→class RetrievalMethod(str, Enum):
    24→    VECTOR = "vector"
    25→    LEXICAL = "lexical"
    26→    HYBRID = "hybrid"
    27→    METADATA = "metadata"
    28→
    29→
    30→@dataclass
    31→class RetrievedChunk:
    32→    """A retrieved chunk with score, provenance, and expanded context."""
    33→    chunk_id: str
    34→    doc_id: str
    35→    content: str
    36→    context: str        # expanded context (neighboring chunks)
    37→    score: float
    38→    source_ref: str
    39→    doc_hash: str | None
    40→    section: str
    41→    chunk_index: int
    42→    retrieval_method: RetrievalMethod
    43→
    44→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    45→        return {
    46→            "chunk_id": self.chunk_id,
    47→            "doc_id": self.doc_id,
    48→            "content": self.content,
    49→            "context": self.context,
    50→            "score": round(self.score, 4),
    51→            "source_ref": self.source_ref,
    52→            "doc_hash": self.doc_hash,
    53→            "section": self.section,
    54→            "chunk_index": self.chunk_index,
    55→            "retrieval_method": self.retrieval_method.value,
    56→        }
    57→
    58→
    59→@dataclass
    60→class RetrievalResult:
    61→    """Full retrieval response with metadata."""
    62→    query: str
    63→    chunks: list[RetrievedChunk]
    64→    retrieved_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    65→    retrieval_method: RetrievalMethod = RetrievalMethod.HYBRID
    66→    total_candidates: int = 0
    67→    context_expanded: bool = True
    68→
    69→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    70→        return {
    71→            "query": self.query,
    72→            "chunks": [c.to_dict() for c in self.chunks],
    73→            "retrieved_at": self.retrieved_at,
    74→            "retrieval_method": self.retrieval_method.value,
    75→            "total_candidates": self.total_candidates,
    76→            "context_expanded": self.context_expanded,
    77→        }
    78→
    79→
    80→EmbedFn = Callable[[str], list[float]]
    81→
    82→
    83→class HybridRetriever:
    84→    """
    85→    Hybrid retriever over a DocumentStore.
    86→
    87→    Lexical path: TF-IDF cosine (no external deps).
    88→    Vector path: cosine similarity on injected embeddings.
    89→    Hybrid: weighted combination of both scores.
    90→    """
    91→
    92→    def __init__(
    93→        self,
    94→        store: DocumentStore,
    95→        embed_fn: EmbedFn | None = None,
    96→        lexical_weight: float = 0.4,
    97→        vector_weight: float = 0.6,
    98→        context_window: int = 1,
    99→    ):
   100→        self.store = store
   101→        self.embed_fn = embed_fn
   102→        self.lexical_weight = lexical_weight
   103→        self.vector_weight = vector_weight
   104→        self.context_window = context_window
   105→
   106→        # Lexical index: chunk_id → term-frequency dict
   107→        self._tf: dict[str, Counter] = {}
   108→        self._idf: dict[str, float] = {}
   109→        self._index_built = False
   110→
   111→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   112→    # Public API
   113→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   114→
   115→    def retrieve(
   116→        self,
   117→        query: str,
   118→        top_k: int = 5,
   119→        method: RetrievalMethod = RetrievalMethod.HYBRID,
   120→        metadata_filter: dict | None = None,
   121→    ) -> RetrievalResult:
   122→        """
   123→        Retrieve top_k chunks for a query using the specified method.
   124→        Always expands context via neighboring chunks.
   125→        """
   126→        all_chunks = self.store.all_chunks()
   127→        if metadata_filter:
   128→            all_chunks = self._apply_metadata_filter(all_chunks, metadata_filter)
   129→
   130→        if not all_chunks:
   131→            return RetrievalResult(query=query, chunks=[], total_candidates=0)
   132→
   133→        if method == RetrievalMethod.LEXICAL:
   134→            scored = self._lexical_score(query, all_chunks)
   135→        elif method == RetrievalMethod.VECTOR and self.embed_fn:
   136→            scored = self._vector_score(query, all_chunks)
   137→        elif method == RetrievalMethod.HYBRID:
   138→            scored = self._hybrid_score(query, all_chunks)
   139→        else:
   140→            # Fallback to lexical
   141→            scored = self._lexical_score(query, all_chunks)
   142→            method = RetrievalMethod.LEXICAL
   143→
   144→        scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
   145→        top = scored[:top_k]
   146→
   147→        results = []
   148→        for chunk, score in top:
   149→            doc = self.store.get_doc(chunk.doc_id)
   150→            expanded = self.store.expand_context(chunk.chunk_id, self.context_window)
   151→            results.append(RetrievedChunk(
   152→                chunk_id=chunk.chunk_id,
   153→                doc_id=chunk.doc_id,
   154→                content=chunk.content,
   155→                context=expanded,
   156→                score=score,
   157→                source_ref=doc.source_uri if doc else "",
   158→                doc_hash=doc.doc_hash if doc else None,
   159→                section=chunk.section,
   160→                chunk_index=chunk.chunk_index,
   161→                retrieval_method=method,
   162→            ))
   163→
   164→        return RetrievalResult(
   165→            query=query,
   166→            chunks=results,
   167→            retrieval_method=method,
   168→            total_candidates=len(all_chunks),
   169→            context_expanded=True,
   170→        )
   171→
   172→    def build_index(self):
   173→        """(Re)build the lexical TF-IDF index over all current chunks."""
   174→        chunks = self.store.all_chunks()
   175→        N = len(chunks)
   176→        if N == 0:
   177→            return
   178→
   179→        self._tf = {}
   180→        df: Counter = Counter()
   181→
   182→        for chunk in chunks:
   183→            tokens = _tokenize(chunk.content)
   184→            tf = Counter(tokens)
   185→            self._tf[chunk.chunk_id] = tf
   186→            for term in set(tokens):
   187→                df[term] += 1
   188→
   189→        self._idf = {
   190→            term: math.log((N + 1) / (count + 1)) + 1
   191→            for term, count in df.items()
   192→        }
   193→        self._index_built = True
   194→
   195→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   196→    # Scoring internals
   197→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   198→
   199→    def _lexical_score(
   200→        self, query: str, chunks: list[DocumentChunk]
   201→    ) -> list[tuple[DocumentChunk, float]]:
   202→        if not self._index_built:
   203→            self.build_index()
   204→
   205→        q_tokens = _tokenize(query)
   206→        if not q_tokens:
   207→            return [(c, 0.0) for c in chunks]
   208→
   209→        q_tf = Counter(q_tokens)
   210→        q_vec = {t: q_tf[t] * self._idf.get(t, 1.0) for t in q_tf}
   211→
   212→        results = []
   213→        for chunk in chunks:
   214→            tf = self._tf.get(chunk.chunk_id, Counter())
   215→            d_vec = {t: tf[t] * self._idf.get(t, 1.0) for t in tf}
   216→            score = _cosine(q_vec, d_vec)
   217→            results.append((chunk, score))
   218→        return results
   219→
   220→    def _vector_score(
   221→        self, query: str, chunks: list[DocumentChunk]
   222→    ) -> list[tuple[DocumentChunk, float]]:
   223→        assert self.embed_fn is not None
   224→        q_emb = self.embed_fn(query)
   225→        results = []
   226→        for chunk in chunks:
   227→            if not chunk.embedding:
   228→                results.append((chunk, 0.0))
   229→            else:
   230→                score = _cosine_vec(q_emb, chunk.embedding)
   231→                results.append((chunk, score))
   232→        return results
   233→
   234→    def _hybrid_score(
   235→        self, query: str, chunks: list[DocumentChunk]
   236→    ) -> list[tuple[DocumentChunk, float]]:
   237→        lex_list = self._lexical_score(query, chunks)
   238→        lex = {c.chunk_id: s for c, s in lex_list}
   239→
   240→        if self.embed_fn:
   241→            vec_list = self._vector_score(query, chunks)
   242→            vec = {c.chunk_id: s for c, s in vec_list}
   243→            results = []
   244→            for chunk in chunks:
   245→                score = (
   246→                    self.lexical_weight * lex.get(chunk.chunk_id, 0.0)
   247→                    + self.vector_weight * vec.get(chunk.chunk_id, 0.0)
   248→                )
   249→                results.append((chunk, score))
   250→        else:
   251→            results = lex_list
   252→
   253→        return results
   254→
   255→    def _apply_metadata_filter(
   256→        self, chunks: list[DocumentChunk], filters: dict
   257→    ) -> list[DocumentChunk]:
   258→        """Filter chunks by doc metadata fields."""
   259→        result = []
   260→        for chunk in chunks:
   261→            doc = self.store.get_doc(chunk.doc_id)
   262→            if doc and all(doc.metadata.get(k) == v for k, v in filters.items()):
   263→                result.append(chunk)
   264→        return result
   265→
   266→
   267→# ------------------------------------------------------------------
   268→# Helpers
   269→# ------------------------------------------------------------------
   270→
   271→def _tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]:
   272→    return re.findall(r'\b[a-z]{2,}\b', text.lower())
   273→
   274→
   275→def _cosine(a: dict[str, float], b: dict[str, float]) -> float:
   276→    dot = sum(a.get(t, 0) * b.get(t, 0) for t in a)
   277→    mag_a = math.sqrt(sum(v ** 2 for v in a.values()))
   278→    mag_b = math.sqrt(sum(v ** 2 for v in b.values()))
   279→    if mag_a == 0 or mag_b == 0:
   280→        return 0.0
   281→    return dot / (mag_a * mag_b)
   282→
   283→
   284→def _cosine_vec(a: list[float], b: list[float]) -> float:
   285→    if len(a) != len(b):
   286→        return 0.0
   287→    dot = sum(x * y for x, y in zip(a, b))
   288→    mag_a = math.sqrt(sum(x ** 2 for x in a))
   289→    mag_b = math.sqrt(sum(y ** 2 for y in b))
   290→    if mag_a == 0 or mag_b == 0:
   291→        return 0.0
   292→    return dot / (mag_a * mag_b)
   293→

1→"""
     2→ADCCL — Anti-Drift Cognitive Control Loop.
     3→
     4→Implements the drift penalty J, verification score V, and claim budgets.
     5→The control loop: Anchor → Skeleton → Ground → Flesh → Verify → Repair/Refuse.
     6→
     7→Phase 5 additions:
     8→  - ClaimBudget is now mandatory answer middleware (verify before emit)
     9→  - VerifierMiddleware enforces budget validity before any answer leaves the layer
    10→  - Low-evidence abstention returns a structured refusal, not raw model text
    11→
    12→Specs: ADCCL_DRIFT_PENALTY, ADCCL_VERIFICATION_GATE, ADCCL_GOAL_CONTRACT
    13→"""
    14→
    15→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    16→from typing import Optional, Callable, Any
    17→from enum import Enum
    18→
    19→
    20→class SupportStatus(Enum):
    21→    SUPPORTED = "supported"
    22→    COMPUTED = "computed"
    23→    HYPOTHETICAL = "hypothetical"
    24→    UNKNOWN = "unknown"
    25→
    26→
    27→@dataclass
    28→class Claim:
    29→    text: str
    30→    support: SupportStatus
    31→    evidence: Optional[str] = None
    32→
    33→
    34→class ClaimBudget:
    35→    """Explicit list of claims the system expects to make."""
    36→
    37→    def __init__(self):
    38→        self.claims: list[Claim] = []
    39→
    40→    def add(self, text: str, support: SupportStatus, evidence: str | None = None):
    41→        self.claims.append(Claim(text, support, evidence))
    42→
    43→    def is_valid(self) -> bool:
    44→        """All supported claims must have evidence."""
    45→        return all(
    46→            c.evidence is not None
    47→            for c in self.claims
    48→            if c.support == SupportStatus.SUPPORTED
    49→        )
    50→
    51→    def grounding_ratio(self) -> float:
    52→        if not self.claims:
    53→            return 0.0
    54→        grounded = sum(1 for c in self.claims
    55→                       if c.support in (SupportStatus.SUPPORTED, SupportStatus.COMPUTED))
    56→        return grounded / len(self.claims)
    57→
    58→
    59→@dataclass
    60→class GoalContract:
    61→    """Canonical structured statement of task, scope, and constraints."""
    62→    task: str
    63→    scope: str = ""
    64→    constraints: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    65→    success_criteria: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    66→    assumptions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    67→    unknowns: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    68→
    69→
    70→class DriftController:
    71→    """ADCCL verification and drift scoring."""
    72→
    73→    def __init__(self, goal: GoalContract, w_s: float = 0.3, w_c: float = 0.4, w_g: float = 0.3):
    74→        self.goal = goal
    75→        self.w_s = w_s
    76→        self.w_c = w_c
    77→        self.w_g = w_g
    78→
    79→    def drift_penalty(self, tokens: list[tuple[float, float, float]]) -> float:
    80→        """Compute J over a sequence of (d_s, d_c, g_t) tuples.
    81→        J = Σ(w_s*d_s + w_c*d_c + w_g*g_t)
    82→        """
    83→        return sum(
    84→            self.w_s * ds + self.w_c * dc + self.w_g * gt
    85→            for ds, dc, gt in tokens
    86→        )
    87→
    88→    def verify(self, response: str, alpha: float = 0.3, beta: float = 0.3) -> dict:
    89→        """Compute verification score V for a completed draft.
    90→        V = 1 - (h/N + α*(1-c) + β*g)
    91→        """
    92→        words = response.split()
    93→        N = max(len(words), 1)
    94→
    95→        # Hallucination proxy: overconfident absolutes
    96→        certainty_words = sum(1 for w in words if w.lower() in
    97→                              {"definitely", "certainly", "absolutely", "always", "never"})
    98→
    99→        # Goal relevance proxy
   100→        goal_words = set(self.goal.task.lower().split())
   101→        response_words = set(response.lower().split())
   102→        coverage = len(goal_words & response_words) / max(len(goal_words), 1)
   103→
   104→        # Drift proxy: excessive hedging
   105→        hedge_count = sum(1 for w in words if w.lower() in
   106→                          {"perhaps", "maybe", "possibly", "might", "could"})
   107→
   108→        h = certainty_words
   109→        c = coverage
   110→        g = hedge_count / N
   111→
   112→        V = 1.0 - (h / N + alpha * (1 - c) + beta * g)
   113→        V = max(0.0, min(1.0, V))
   114→
   115→        passed = V > 0.4
   116→
   117→        return {
   118→            "V": round(V, 4),
   119→            "length": len(words),
   120→            "hallucination_signals": certainty_words,
   121→            "hedge_signals": hedge_count,
   122→            "goal_relevance": round(coverage, 4),
   123→            "passed": passed,
   124→            "outcome": "verified" if passed else "uncertain",
   125→        }
   126→
   127→
   128→class VerifierMiddleware:
   129→    """
   130→    Mandatory answer middleware: no answer leaves ADCCL without passing
   131→    budget validity + verification score threshold.
   132→
   133→    Usage:
   134→        mw = VerifierMiddleware(goal, budget)
   135→        result = mw.check(raw_text, verifier_dict)
   136→        if result["allowed"]:
   137→            emit(raw_text)
   138→        else:
   139→            emit(result["fallback"])
   140→    """
   141→
   142→    def __init__(
   143→        self,
   144→        goal: GoalContract,
   145→        budget: "ClaimBudget | None" = None,
   146→        verify_threshold: float = 0.4,
   147→        grounding_threshold: float = 0.2,
   148→    ):
   149→        self.goal = goal
   150→        self.budget = budget
   151→        self.verify_threshold = verify_threshold
   152→        self.grounding_threshold = grounding_threshold
   153→        self._controller = DriftController(goal)
   154→
   155→    def check(
   156→        self,
   157→        response: str,
   158→        verifier_dict: dict | None = None,
   159→        grounding_ratio: float = 1.0,
   160→    ) -> dict:
   161→        """
   162→        Returns a gate decision dict:
   163→          allowed: bool
   164→          V: float
   165→          reason: str
   166→          fallback: str  (populated when allowed=False)
   167→        """
   168→        v = verifier_dict or self._controller.verify(response)
   169→
   170→        # Budget gate
   171→        budget_ok = True
   172→        budget_reason = ""
   173→        if self.budget:
   174→            if not self.budget.is_valid():
   175→                budget_ok = False
   176→                budget_reason = "ClaimBudget has unsupported claims without evidence"
   177→            if self.budget.grounding_ratio() < self.grounding_threshold:
   178→                budget_ok = False
   179→                budget_reason = (
   180→                    f"Grounding ratio {self.budget.grounding_ratio():.2f} "
   181→                    f"below threshold {self.grounding_threshold}"
   182→                )
   183→
   184→        # Verifier gate
   185→        verifier_ok = v.get("V", 0) >= self.verify_threshold
   186→        verifier_reason = "" if verifier_ok else f"V={v.get('V', 0):.3f} below threshold"
   187→
   188→        allowed = budget_ok and verifier_ok
   189→
   190→        if allowed:
   191→            return {"allowed": True, "V": v["V"], "reason": "pass", "fallback": ""}
   192→
   193→        reasons = [r for r in [budget_reason, verifier_reason] if r]
   194→        fallback = (
   195→            "This response was withheld by the ADCCL verifier. "
   196→            "Reason: " + "; ".join(reasons) + ". "
   197→            "Please provide source evidence or rephrase the query."
   198→        )
   199→        return {
   200→            "allowed": False,
   201→            "V": v.get("V", 0),
   202→            "reason": "; ".join(reasons),
   203→            "fallback": fallback,
   204→        }
   205→

1→"""
     2→OmegA Unified Request Runtime — One execution path for every request.
     3→
     4→Lifecycle: ingest → retrieve → plan → generate → verify → gate → log
     5→
     6→Every request gets a run_id, stage timing, and structured trace.
     7→No answer leaves the system without verifier + gate participation.
     8→
     9→Architecture: Cross-cutting (AEGIS + AEON + ADCCL + MYELIN)
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import hashlib
    13→import time
    14→import uuid
    15→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    16→from enum import Enum
    17→from typing import Any
    18→
    19→from omega.phylactery import Phylactery
    20→from omega.envelope import RunEnvelope, ActionGate, ActionClass
    21→from omega.risk_gate import RiskGate
    22→from omega.drift import DriftController, GoalContract, VerifierMiddleware
    23→from omega.memory import MemoryGraph, Stratum, WritePolicy
    24→from omega.docstore import DocumentStore, DocFormat
    25→from omega.retrieval import HybridRetriever
    26→from omega.answer import AnswerBuilder, AnswerObject
    27→from omega.capabilities import CapabilityRegistry, default_capabilities
    28→from omega.providers.base import (
    29→    ProviderRouter, ProviderRequest, ProviderResponse, ProviderStatus, RoutingPolicy,
    30→)
    31→from omega.providers.ollama import OllamaProvider
    32→from omega.providers.openai import OpenAIProvider
    33→from omega.providers.anthropic import AnthropicProvider
    34→from omega.providers.google import GoogleProvider
    35→
    36→
    37→class RuntimeStage(str, Enum):
    38→    INIT = "init"
    39→    RISK_GATE = "risk_gate"
    40→    RETRIEVE = "retrieve"
    41→    PLAN = "plan"
    42→    GENERATE = "generate"
    43→    VERIFY = "verify"
    44→    ANSWER_BUILD = "answer_build"
    45→    ACTION_GATE = "action_gate"
    46→    COMMIT = "commit"
    47→    COMPLETE = "complete"
    48→    BLOCKED = "blocked"
    49→    ERROR = "error"
    50→
    51→
    52→@dataclass
    53→class StageTrace:
    54→    """Timing and status for a single pipeline stage."""
    55→    stage: RuntimeStage
    56→    started_at: float = 0.0
    57→    elapsed_ms: float = 0.0
    58→    status: str = "pending"
    59→    details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    60→
    61→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    62→        return {
    63→            "stage": self.stage.value,
    64→            "elapsed_ms": round(self.elapsed_ms, 2),
    65→            "status": self.status,
    66→            "details": self.details,
    67→        }
    68→
    69→
    70→@dataclass
    71→class RunTrace:
    72→    """Full structured trace for a single runtime execution."""
    73→    run_id: str
    74→    task: str
    75→    stages: list[StageTrace] = field(default_factory=list)
    76→    total_elapsed_ms: float = 0.0
    77→    final_stage: RuntimeStage = RuntimeStage.INIT
    78→    provider_name: str = ""
    79→    model: str = ""
    80→    risk_score: float = 0.0
    81→    risk_allowed: bool = True
    82→    verification_V: float = 0.0
    83→    verification_passed: bool = False
    84→    answer_mode: str = ""
    85→    gate_allowed: bool = True
    86→    error: str = ""
    87→
    88→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    89→        return {
    90→            "run_id": self.run_id,
    91→            "task": self.task[:200],
    92→            "stages": [s.to_dict() for s in self.stages],
    93→            "total_elapsed_ms": round(self.total_elapsed_ms, 1),
    94→            "final_stage": self.final_stage.value,
    95→            "provider_name": self.provider_name,
    96→            "model": self.model,
    97→            "risk_score": round(self.risk_score, 4),
    98→            "risk_allowed": self.risk_allowed,
    99→            "verification_V": round(self.verification_V, 4),
   100→            "verification_passed": self.verification_passed,
   101→            "answer_mode": self.answer_mode,
   102→            "gate_allowed": self.gate_allowed,
   103→            "error": self.error,
   104→        }
   105→
   106→
   107→@dataclass
   108→class RuntimeResult:
   109→    """Top-level result of a RuntimeOrchestrator.run() call."""
   110→    trace: RunTrace
   111→    answer: AnswerObject | None = None
   112→    raw_response: str = ""
   113→    provider_response: ProviderResponse | None = None
   114→
   115→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
   116→        return {
   117→            "trace": self.trace.to_dict(),
   118→            "answer": self.answer.to_dict() if self.answer else None,
   119→            "raw_response": self.raw_response[:500],
   120→        }
   121→
   122→    @property
   123→    def text(self) -> str:
   124→        if self.answer:
   125→            return self.answer.text
   126→        return self.raw_response
   127→
   128→
   129→class RuntimeOrchestrator:
   130→    """
   131→    Unified execution path for all OmegA requests.
   132→
   133→    Composes: RiskGate, HybridRetriever, ProviderRouter, DriftController,
   134→    AnswerBuilder, ActionGate, MemoryGraph.
   135→
   136→    Every request flows through: risk → retrieve → generate → verify → gate → commit.
   137→    """
   138→
   139→    DEFAULT_DOCTRINE = "I am what I am, and I will be what I will be."
   140→
   141→    def __init__(
   142→        self,
   143→        name: str = "OmegA",
   144→        doctrine: str | None = None,
   145→        docstore: DocumentStore | None = None,
   146→        memory: MemoryGraph | None = None,
   147→    ):
   148→        self.name = name
   149→        self.phylactery = Phylactery(doctrine or self.DEFAULT_DOCTRINE)
   150→        self.risk_gate = RiskGate()
   151→        self.action_gate = ActionGate()
   152→        self.memory = memory or MemoryGraph()
   153→        self.docstore = docstore or DocumentStore()
   154→        self.retriever = HybridRetriever(self.docstore)
   155→        self.answer_builder = AnswerBuilder()
   156→        self.capabilities = CapabilityRegistry()
   157→        self.router = ProviderRouter()
   158→
   159→        # Register default capabilities
   160→        for cap in default_capabilities():
   161→            self.capabilities.register(cap)
   162→
   163→        # Register all providers (each is safe without credentials)
   164→        self.router.register(OllamaProvider())
   165→        self.router.register(OpenAIProvider())
   166→        self.router.register(AnthropicProvider())
   167→        self.router.register(GoogleProvider())
   168→        self.router.set_preference(["ollama", "openai", "google", "anthropic"])
   169→
   170→        self.identity_kernel = {
   171→            "name": self.name,
   172→            "doctrine": self.phylactery.doctrine,
   173→            "phylactery_head": self.phylactery.head,
   174→            "hard_constraints": [
   175→                "Never identify as the underlying LLM provider",
   176→                "Never fabricate evidence",
   177→                "Always distinguish known from unknown",
   178→            ],
   179→        }
   180→
   181→    def run(
   182→        self,
   183→        task: str,
   184→        model: str = "",
   185→        provider: str | None = None,
   186→        temperature: float = 0.3,
   187→        max_tokens: int = 512,
   188→        routing_policy: RoutingPolicy = RoutingPolicy.FALLBACK,
   189→        context: str = "runtime",
   190→    ) -> RuntimeResult:
   191→        """Execute the full OmegA runtime pipeline for a single task."""
   192→        run_id = f"run_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   193→        trace = RunTrace(run_id=run_id, task=task)
   194→        global_start = time.time()
   195→
   196→        # ── Stage 1: Risk Gate ────────────────────────────────────────
   197→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.RISK_GATE)
   198→        allowed, R = self.risk_gate.gate(task)
   199→        trace.risk_score = R
   200→        trace.risk_allowed = allowed
   201→        stage.details = {"risk_score": R, "allowed": allowed}
   202→
   203→        if not allowed:
   204→            stage.status = "blocked"
   205→            self._end_stage(stage)
   206→            trace.stages.append(stage)
   207→            trace.final_stage = RuntimeStage.BLOCKED
   208→            trace.total_elapsed_ms = (time.time() - global_start) * 1000
   209→            return RuntimeResult(
   210→                trace=trace,
   211→                raw_response="[BLOCKED] Request rejected by AEGIS risk gate.",
   212→            )
   213→
   214→        stage.status = "pass"
   215→        self._end_stage(stage)
   216→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   217→
   218→        # ── Stage 2: Retrieve ─────────────────────────────────────────
   219→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.RETRIEVE)
   220→        cap_check = self.capabilities.check("cap.docstore.retrieve", context)
   221→        if cap_check.allowed:
   222→            retrieval_result = self.retriever.retrieve(task, top_k=5)
   223→            chunks = retrieval_result.chunks
   224→            stage.details = {"chunks_found": len(chunks), "total_candidates": retrieval_result.total_candidates}
   225→        else:
   226→            chunks = []
   227→            stage.details = {"skipped": True, "reason": cap_check.reason}
   228→        stage.status = "done"
   229→        self._end_stage(stage)
   230→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   231→
   232→        # ── Stage 3: Plan ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   233→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.PLAN)
   234→        goal = GoalContract(task=task)
   235→        envelope = RunEnvelope(identity_kernel=self.identity_kernel, goal_contract=task)
   236→        system_prompt = envelope.to_system_prompt()
   237→        if chunks:
   238→            context_text = "\n\n".join(c.context for c in chunks[:3])
   239→            system_prompt += f"\n\nRelevant sources:\n{context_text}"
   240→        stage.status = "done"
   241→        self._end_stage(stage)
   242→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   243→
   244→        # ── Stage 4: Generate ─────────────────────────────────────────
   245→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.GENERATE)
   246→        cap_check = self.capabilities.check("cap.llm.generate", context)
   247→        if not cap_check.allowed:
   248→            stage.status = "blocked"
   249→            stage.details = {"reason": cap_check.reason}
   250→            self._end_stage(stage)
   251→            trace.stages.append(stage)
   252→            trace.final_stage = RuntimeStage.BLOCKED
   253→            trace.total_elapsed_ms = (time.time() - global_start) * 1000
   254→            return RuntimeResult(trace=trace, raw_response="[BLOCKED] LLM generation capability not allowed.")
   255→
   256→        provider_request = ProviderRequest(
   257→            prompt=task,
   258→            system=system_prompt,
   259→            model=model,
   260→            temperature=temperature,
   261→            max_tokens=max_tokens,
   262→            run_id=run_id,
   263→        )
   264→        provider_response = self.router.route(
   265→            provider_request,
   266→            policy=routing_policy,
   267→            provider_name=provider,
   268→        )
   269→        trace.provider_name = provider_response.provider_name
   270→        trace.model = provider_response.model
   271→        stage.details = {
   272→            "provider": provider_response.provider_name,
   273→            "model": provider_response.model,
   274→            "status": provider_response.status.value,
   275→            "latency_ms": provider_response.latency_ms,
   276→        }
   277→
   278→        if not provider_response.ok:
   279→            stage.status = "error"
   280→            trace.error = provider_response.error_message
   281→            self._end_stage(stage)
   282→            trace.stages.append(stage)
   283→            trace.final_stage = RuntimeStage.ERROR
   284→            trace.total_elapsed_ms = (time.time() - global_start) * 1000
   285→            return RuntimeResult(
   286→                trace=trace,
   287→                raw_response=f"[PROVIDER ERROR] {provider_response.error_message}",
   288→                provider_response=provider_response,
   289→            )
   290→
   291→        raw_text = provider_response.text

   294→        trace.stages.append(stage)

   344→        )
   345→        from omega.envelope import ApprovalStatus
   346→        trace.gate_allowed = gate_env.approval.status == ApprovalStatus.APPROVED

1→"""
     2→MYELIN — Path-Dependent Graph Memory.
     3→
     4→Sparse graph with bundled edge signals, strata-based decay,
     5→and hardening through successful retrieval.
     6→
     7→Phase 6 additions:
     8→  - WritePolicy enum separates canonical / working / inferred / speculative writes
     9→  - MemoryGraph.write() enforces policy before committing any node
    10→  - Canonical nodes are immutable once written (upgrade requires explicit promotion)
    11→
    12→Specs: MYELIN_GRAPH_MEMORY, MYELIN_EDGE_BUNDLES, MYELIN_SPATIAL_STABILIZATION
    13→"""
    14→
    15→import time
    16→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    17→from enum import Enum
    18→from typing import Literal
    19→
    20→
    21→class Stratum(Enum):
    22→    CANONICAL = "canonical"
    23→    OPERATIONAL = "operational"
    24→    EPISODIC = "episodic"
    25→    SPECULATIVE = "speculative"
    26→
    27→
    28→STRATUM_DECAY = {
    29→    Stratum.CANONICAL: 0.0,
    30→    Stratum.OPERATIONAL: 0.01,
    31→    Stratum.EPISODIC: 0.05,
    32→    Stratum.SPECULATIVE: 0.2,
    33→}
    34→
    35→
    36→class WritePolicy(Enum):
    37→    """
    38→    Memory write policy — determines how a node is treated at write time.
    39→
    40→    CANONICAL   — source-verified facts; immutable once written.
    41→    WORKING     — active task context; mutable, decays at end of session.
    42→    INFERRED    — derived from context; mutable, lower confidence.
    43→    SPECULATIVE — hypotheses; highest decay, must be promoted to persist.
    44→    """
    45→    CANONICAL = "canonical"
    46→    WORKING = "working"
    47→    INFERRED = "inferred"
    48→    SPECULATIVE = "speculative"
    49→
    50→
    51→# Map WritePolicy to the equivalent Stratum for storage
    52→POLICY_TO_STRATUM: dict[WritePolicy, Stratum] = {
    53→    WritePolicy.CANONICAL: Stratum.CANONICAL,
    54→    WritePolicy.WORKING: Stratum.OPERATIONAL,
    55→    WritePolicy.INFERRED: Stratum.EPISODIC,
    56→    WritePolicy.SPECULATIVE: Stratum.SPECULATIVE,
    57→}
    58→
    59→
    60→@dataclass
    61→class MemoryNode:
    62→    id: str
    63→    content: str
    64→    stratum: Stratum = Stratum.EPISODIC
    65→    embedding: list[float] = field(default_factory=list)
    66→    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    67→    access_count: int = 0
    68→
    69→
    70→@dataclass
    71→class EdgeBundle:
    72→    """Bundled relationship between two memory nodes."""
    73→    source: str
    74→    target: str
    75→    semantic: float = 0.0       # s_ij: semantic similarity
    76→    coactivation: int = 0       # c_ij: co-retrieval count
    77→    retrieval_util: float = 0.5 # q_ij: retrieval utility
    78→    staleness: float = 0.0      # δ_ij: time since last access
    79→
    80→    def harden(self, reward: float, lam: float = 0.1):
    81→        """q(t+1) = (1-λ)*q(t) + λ*r"""
    82→        self.retrieval_util = (1 - lam) * self.retrieval_util + lam * reward
    83→        if reward > 0:
    84→            self.coactivation += 1
    85→            self.staleness = 0.0
    86→
    87→    def decay(self, dt: float, rate: float):
    88→        """Apply time-based decay to staleness."""
    89→        self.staleness += dt * rate
    90→
    91→
    92→class MemoryGraph:
    93→    """Sparse path-dependent memory graph."""
    94→
    95→    def __init__(self):
    96→        self.nodes: dict[str, MemoryNode] = {}
    97→        self.edges: dict[tuple[str, str], EdgeBundle] = {}
    98→
    99→    def write(
   100→        self,
   101→        id: str,
   102→        content: str,
   103→        policy: WritePolicy = WritePolicy.WORKING,
   104→        embedding: list[float] | None = None,
   105→        source_ref: str | None = None,
   106→    ) -> MemoryNode:
   107→        """
   108→        Policy-aware write. Enforces:
   109→        - CANONICAL nodes cannot be overwritten once committed.
   110→        - SPECULATIVE nodes are flagged and subject to automatic decay.
   111→        - source_ref is required for CANONICAL writes.
   112→        """
   113→        stratum = POLICY_TO_STRATUM[policy]
   114→
   115→        if policy == WritePolicy.CANONICAL:
   116→            if id in self.nodes and self.nodes[id].stratum == Stratum.CANONICAL:
   117→                # Canonical nodes are immutable — return existing without overwrite
   118→                return self.nodes[id]
   119→            if not source_ref:
   120→                raise ValueError(
   121→                    f"CANONICAL write for node '{id}' requires a source_ref."
   122→                )
   123→
   124→        node = MemoryNode(
   125→            id=id,
   126→            content=content,
   127→            stratum=stratum,
   128→            embedding=embedding or [],
   129→        )
   130→        self.nodes[id] = node
   131→        return node
   132→
   133→    def promote(self, id: str, new_policy: WritePolicy, source_ref: str | None = None) -> MemoryNode:
   134→        """Promote a node to a higher-trust policy stratum."""
   135→        node = self.nodes.get(id)
   136→        if not node:
   137→            raise KeyError(f"Node '{id}' not found in memory graph")
   138→        if new_policy == WritePolicy.CANONICAL and not source_ref:
   139→            raise ValueError("Promotion to CANONICAL requires a source_ref.")
   140→        node.stratum = POLICY_TO_STRATUM[new_policy]
   141→        return node
   142→
   143→    def add_node(self, id: str, content: str, stratum: Stratum = Stratum.EPISODIC,
   144→                 embedding: list[float] | None = None) -> MemoryNode:
   145→        node = MemoryNode(id=id, content=content, stratum=stratum,
   146→                          embedding=embedding or [])
   147→        self.nodes[id] = node
   148→        return node
   149→
   150→    def add_edge(self, source: str, target: str, semantic: float = 0.0) -> EdgeBundle:
   151→        edge = EdgeBundle(source=source, target=target, semantic=semantic)
   152→        self.edges[(source, target)] = edge
   153→        return edge
   154→
   155→    def retrieve(self, node_id: str) -> MemoryNode | None:
   156→        """Retrieve a node and record access."""
   157→        node = self.nodes.get(node_id)
   158→        if node:
   159→            node.access_count += 1
   160→        return node
   161→
   162→    def harden_path(self, path: list[str], reward: float = 1.0):
   163→        """Harden all edges along a retrieval path."""
   164→        for i in range(len(path) - 1):
   165→            key = (path[i], path[i + 1])
   166→            if key in self.edges:
   167→                self.edges[key].harden(reward)
   168→
   169→    def apply_decay(self, dt: float = 1.0):
   170→        """Apply stratum-based decay to all edges."""
   171→        for (src, _), edge in self.edges.items():
   172→            src_node = self.nodes.get(src)
   173→            if src_node:
   174→                rate = STRATUM_DECAY.get(src_node.stratum, 0.05)
   175→                edge.decay(dt, rate)
   176→
   177→    def neighbors(self, node_id: str) -> list[tuple[str, EdgeBundle]]:
   178→        """Get all neighbors of a node with their edge bundles."""
   179→        result = []
   180→        for (src, tgt), edge in self.edges.items():
   181→            if src == node_id:
   182→                result.append((tgt, edge))
   183→            elif tgt == node_id:
   184→                result.append((src, edge))
   185→        return result
   186→
   187→    @property
   188→    def node_count(self) -> int:
   189→        return len(self.nodes)
   190→
   191→    @property
   192→    def edge_count(self) -> int:
   193→        return len(self.edges)
   194→

"""
Claim Graph + Evidence Linking — Structured claim tracking with contradiction detection.

Every answer's claims are tracked as nodes in a directed graph.
Evidence edges link claims to their support/contradiction relationships.
Uncertainty propagates through contradictions.

Architecture: ADCCL Phase 5 — Claim Accountability
"""

import time
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from omega.answer import AnswerObject
    from omega.retrieval import RetrievedChunk


class ClaimType(str, Enum):
    SUPPORTED = "supported"
    INFERRED = "inferred"
    CONTRADICTED = "contradicted"
    UNRESOLVED = "unresolved"


class EdgeRelation(str, Enum):
    SUPPORTS = "supports"
    CONTRADICTS = "contradicts"
    QUALIFIES = "qualifies"
    SUPERSEDES = "supersedes"


@dataclass
class ClaimNode:
    claim_id: str
    text: str
    claim_type: ClaimType
    support_strength: float  # 0-1
    grounding_strength: float  # 0-1
    source_refs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "claim_id": self.claim_id,
            "text": self.text,
            "claim_type": self.claim_type.value,
            "support_strength": round(self.support_strength, 4),
            "grounding_strength": round(self.grounding_strength, 4),
            "source_refs": self.source_refs,
            "created_at": self.created_at,
        }


@dataclass
class EvidenceEdge:
    edge_id: str
    source_claim_id: str
    target_claim_id: str
    relation: EdgeRelation
    weight: float  # 0-1
    source_ref: str = ""
    excerpt: str = ""

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "edge_id": self.edge_id,
            "source_claim_id": self.source_claim_id,
            "target_claim_id": self.target_claim_id,
            "relation": self.relation.value,
            "weight": round(self.weight, 4),
            "source_ref": self.source_ref,
            "excerpt": self.excerpt[:200],
        }


class ClaimGraph:
    """
    Directed graph of claims with evidence edges.
    Tracks support, contradiction, qualification, and supersession.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.claims: dict[str, ClaimNode] = {}
        self.edges: list[EvidenceEdge] = []

    def add_claim(self, claim: ClaimNode) -> ClaimNode:
        self.claims[claim.claim_id] = claim
        return claim

    def add_edge(self, edge: EvidenceEdge) -> EvidenceEdge:
        self.edges.append(edge)
        return edge

    def get_contradictions(self) -> list[tuple[ClaimNode, ClaimNode, EvidenceEdge]]:
        """Return all contradiction triples (source_claim, target_claim, edge)."""
        result = []
        for edge in self.edges:
            if edge.relation == EdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS:
                src = self.claims.get(edge.source_claim_id)
                tgt = self.claims.get(edge.target_claim_id)
                if src and tgt:
                    result.append((src, tgt, edge))
        return result

    def get_unresolved(self) -> list[ClaimNode]:
        """Return all claims marked UNRESOLVED."""
        return [c for c in self.claims.values() if c.claim_type == ClaimType.UNRESOLVED]

    def grounding_ratio(self) -> float:
        """Fraction of claims that are grounded (SUPPORTED with grounding_strength > 0.3)."""
        if not self.claims:
            return 0.0
        grounded = sum(
            1 for c in self.claims.values()
            if c.claim_type == ClaimType.SUPPORTED and c.grounding_strength > 0.3
        )
        return grounded / len(self.claims)

    def propagate_uncertainty(self) -> None:
        """
        Reduce support_strength of claims that are contradicted.
        For each contradiction edge, the target claim's support_strength
        is reduced proportionally to the edge weight and the source claim's
        support_strength.
        """
        for edge in self.edges:
            if edge.relation == EdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS:
                target = self.claims.get(edge.target_claim_id)
                source = self.claims.get(edge.source_claim_id)
                if target and source:
                    penalty = edge.weight * source.support_strength * 0.5
                    target.support_strength = max(0.0, target.support_strength - penalty)
                    if target.support_strength < 0.2:
                        target.claim_type = ClaimType.CONTRADICTED

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "claims": {cid: c.to_dict() for cid, c in self.claims.items()},
            "edges": [e.to_dict() for e in self.edges],
            "grounding_ratio": round(self.grounding_ratio(), 4),
            "total_claims": len(self.claims),
            "total_edges": len(self.edges),
            "contradictions": len(self.get_contradictions()),
            "unresolved": len(self.get_unresolved()),
        }

    @classmethod
    def from_retrieval_and_answer(
        cls,
        answer: "AnswerObject",
        chunks: list["RetrievedChunk"],
    ) -> "ClaimGraph":
        """
        Build a ClaimGraph from an AnswerObject and its source chunks.

        Strategy:
        - Split answer text into sentences as candidate claims.
        - Each chunk becomes a source evidence node.
        - Match claims to chunks by keyword overlap to create SUPPORTS edges.
        - Claims with no chunk support are marked UNRESOLVED.
        - Claims with strong chunk support are SUPPORTED.
        - Claims with weak support are INFERRED.
        """
        graph = cls()

        # Build chunk-level evidence claims
        chunk_claim_ids: dict[str, str] = {}
        for chunk in chunks:
            cid = str(uuid.uuid4())
            node = ClaimNode(
                claim_id=cid,
                text=chunk.content[:300],
                claim_type=ClaimType.SUPPORTED,
                support_strength=min(1.0, chunk.score),
                grounding_strength=min(1.0, chunk.score),
                source_refs=[chunk.source_ref],
            )
            graph.add_claim(node)
            chunk_claim_ids[chunk.chunk_id] = cid

        # Split answer into sentence-level claims
        sentences = _split_sentences(answer.text)
        answer_claim_ids: list[str] = []

        for sentence in sentences:
            if len(sentence.strip()) < 10:
                continue
            acid = str(uuid.uuid4())
            # Score support by keyword overlap with chunks
            best_score = 0.0
            best_chunk_id = None
            sentence_words = set(sentence.lower().split())

            for chunk in chunks:
                chunk_words = set(chunk.content.lower().split())
                if not chunk_words:
                    continue
                overlap = len(sentence_words & chunk_words) / max(len(sentence_words), 1)
                if overlap > best_score:
                    best_score = overlap
                    best_chunk_id = chunk.chunk_id

            # Classify claim
            if best_score >= 0.3:
                claim_type = ClaimType.SUPPORTED
                support_strength = min(1.0, best_score * 1.5)
                grounding_strength = min(1.0, best_score * 1.2)
            elif best_score >= 0.1:
                claim_type = ClaimType.INFERRED
                support_strength = best_score
                grounding_strength = best_score * 0.5
            else:
                claim_type = ClaimType.UNRESOLVED
                support_strength = 0.0
                grounding_strength = 0.0

            source_refs = []
            if best_chunk_id:
                for chunk in chunks:
                    if chunk.chunk_id == best_chunk_id:
                        source_refs = [chunk.source_ref]
                        break

            node = ClaimNode(
                claim_id=acid,
                text=sentence.strip(),
                claim_type=claim_type,
                support_strength=support_strength,
                grounding_strength=grounding_strength,
                source_refs=source_refs,
            )
            graph.add_claim(node)
            answer_claim_ids.append(acid)

            # Create evidence edge from chunk to answer claim
            if best_chunk_id and best_chunk_id in chunk_claim_ids:
                edge = EvidenceEdge(
                    edge_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                    source_claim_id=chunk_claim_ids[best_chunk_id],
                    target_claim_id=acid,
                    relation=EdgeRelation.SUPPORTS,
                    weight=best_score,
                    source_ref=source_refs[0] if source_refs else "",
                    excerpt=sentence[:200],
                )
                graph.add_edge(edge)

        # Detect contradictions: answer claims that reference conflicting chunks
        # Two chunks contradict if they both support the same answer claim
        # but have very different content (low mutual overlap)
        for i, chunk_a in enumerate(chunks):
            for chunk_b in chunks[i + 1:]:
                words_a = set(chunk_a.content.lower().split())
                words_b = set(chunk_b.content.lower().split())
                if not words_a or not words_b:
                    continue
                mutual = len(words_a & words_b) / max(len(words_a | words_b), 1)
                # If chunks are very different but both scored, flag contradiction
                if mutual < 0.1 and chunk_a.score > 0.3 and chunk_b.score > 0.3:
                    cid_a = chunk_claim_ids.get(chunk_a.chunk_id)
                    cid_b = chunk_claim_ids.get(chunk_b.chunk_id)
                    if cid_a and cid_b:
                        edge = EvidenceEdge(
                            edge_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
                            source_claim_id=cid_a,
                            target_claim_id=cid_b,
                            relation=EdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS,
                            weight=1.0 - mutual,
                            source_ref=chunk_a.source_ref,
                            excerpt=f"Low overlap ({mutual:.2f}) between high-scoring chunks",
                        )
                        graph.add_edge(edge)

        return graph


def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
    """Split text into sentences on period, question mark, exclamation, or newline."""
    import re
    # Split on sentence-ending punctuation followed by space or end
    parts = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+|\n+', text)
    return [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
